steps.title: Identify what the text accomplishes
steps.content: The text opens by stating the study's conclusion, then immediately shows how researchers tested it: the experimental design (two groups with different tasks) and the result. The passage explains both the finding and the path the research team took to reach it.

steps.title: Choose the choice that matches
steps.content: Choice B says the text explains a study's conclusion and how researchers arrived at that conclusion—exactly what happens here. The other three misidentify the purpose: A suggests the text critiques the study's design; C claims it argues researchers were surprised; D treats it as defending a pre-existing belief rather than reporting a new finding. That matches choice B — B is correct.
